# Nullzeit

> Nullzeit is a decompression secondary display for the Apple Watch Ultra. It is
> NOT a certified dive computer (no EN 13319 approval, no redundancy) — it is a
> development beta for divers who test it next to an independent, certified dive
> computer. If the two displays disagree, the certified computer wins.

Key facts:

- Reads live depth, dive time and water temperature from the Apple Watch Ultra's
  native depth sensor; no internet, phone or account needed during the dive.
- Runs a ZH-L16C tissue model (Bühlmann, gradient factors): no-deco limit,
  decompression stops, ceiling, time-to-surface, safety-stop guidance,
  ascent-rate and depth-limit warnings with haptics.
- Currently limited to Apple's publicly available shallow-depth API (6 m). The
  stated roadmap: Apple development approval for 40 m, then EN 13319
  certification and App Store distribution as a real dive computer.
- Independent project by Matthias Schmid, a certified diver and software
  engineer with an automotive functional-safety background. Not affiliated with
  Apple Inc.
- Beta access is requested via GitHub issues; the site is in English and German.

## Pages

- [Home (English)](https://nullzeit.app/): what Nullzeit does today, what it is
  not, the certification roadmap, and how to join the beta.
- [Home (Deutsch)](https://nullzeit.app/de/): German version of the home page.
- [Manual](https://nullzeit.app/manual/): the full user manual — dive start
  modes, screens, warnings, tissue model settings, logbook, troubleshooting.
- [Imprint](https://nullzeit.app/imprint/)
- [Privacy](https://nullzeit.app/privacy/)
